title: Brake before the drop
summary: Descending control starts before gravity wins the first meter.
body: Correct answer: reduce speed before the slope runs away with you.
Why: once the hill adds momentum, control gets harder and hotter.
Trap: speed on descent treats gravity like a harmless lane assist.
Reclaim: name the grade, slow early, and keep the car calm.

title: Engine braking shares the load
summary: A lower gear helps the brakes instead of forcing them to do everything.
body: Correct answer: use the drivetrain to help hold speed down.
Why: on long grades, brake heat is the hidden enemy.
Trap: brake fade bait makes more pedal sound like more control.
Reclaim: downshift early and let the gear carry some of the load.

title: Hairpins demand early slowing
summary: A tight curve rewards patience long before the steering wheel turns hard.
body: Correct answer: slow before the curve closes on you.
Why: a hairpin leaves little room for late corrections.
Trap: late turn-in asks the tires to save a line that is already gone.
Reclaim: brake early, steer smoothly, and choose the clean line.

title: Fog demands a slower approach
summary: You cannot drive safely beyond what you can actually see.
body: Correct answer: slow to the visible field and widen the gap.
Why: fog removes the preview that makes speed feel safe.
Trap: fog overspeed is the choice to trust a road you cannot read.
Reclaim: read the edge line, breathe, and move within sight.

title: Following distance buys recovery
summary: Space is the only thing that makes a mountain surprise manageable.
body: Correct answer: keep more room behind the vehicle ahead.
Why: a downhill stop grows more urgent and more expensive.
Trap: too little gap turns a normal brake into a chain reaction.
Reclaim: leave room, watch the brake lights, and buy time.

title: The shoulder is not a guess
summary: Edge space must be evaluated, not assumed.
body: Correct answer: treat the shoulder as controlled recovery space only.
Why: an edge can be narrow, loose, or unsafe under stress.
Trap: shoulder panic turns a recovery decision into a new mistake.
Reclaim: assess the surface, keep the wheel calm, and recover deliberately.

title: Gravel reduces grip
summary: Loose surface requires softer inputs and slower change.
body: Correct answer: ease the steering and braking so the tires can bite.
Why: loose stones move before the car does.
Trap: gravel commitment is the fantasy that grip stays the same.
Reclaim: reduce load, stabilize the wheel, then ask for more.

title: The apex is a choice
summary: A curve is managed by choosing the point, not by attacking it.
body: Correct answer: pick the line that leaves the car stable at the bend.
Why: the best apex is the one that makes the exit safe.
Trap: treating the apex like a target encourages rushing.
Reclaim: slow first, then choose the point that preserves control.
